description_long: This object is a large, rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running lengthwise across its entire width. The grooves are deep and sharply defined, giving the block a ribbed appearance from above and from the sides. The block is solid and robust, with a matte, dark stone finish that suggests durability and resistance to weathering. Its sides are smooth and unadorned, with the grooves only present on the top surface. The overall color is a deep charcoal or black, with subtle variations in tone that hint at a natural stone material. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of modern landscaping features.
description: A large, dark stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a ribbed and robust appearance. The block is solid, rectangular, and typically used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the ribbed pattern running vertically.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the flat face and horizontal grooves on the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: modern urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a leveled gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with minor speckling.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
